title: hypericum × desetangsii nothovar. carinthiacum
intro: Hypericum × desetangsii nothovar. carinthiacum, also known as the Carinthian St. John's Wort, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Hypericum andyense and Hypericum montanum. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Carinthian St. John's Wort are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of insomnia, nervousness, and digestive issues.
